title: Agent Contract Schema
description: Validates the output of SwarmPlanner decomposition — the contract file written to .antigravity/contracts/<threadId>.json that defines a worker agent's scope, lane assignments, and execution bounds. This is the primary handoff artifact between the planner and the worker executor.

threadId.description: Globally unique thread/run identifier. Convention: ISO-8601 timestamp with millis + optional suffix. Used as the contract filename: .antigravity/contracts/<threadId>.json

role.description: Human-readable role label for this worker (e.g. 'Frontend Developer', 'SEO Content Writer', 'API Implementer'). Used for logging and display.

taskDescription.description: Full natural-language task description passed to the worker as its primary directive. Should include acceptance criteria, context, and expected output format.

allowedDirectories.description: Absolute or repo-relative directory paths the worker is permitted to write to. Lane assignment — the worker may create/modify files here. Paths should end with / to indicate directories.

readOnlyDirectories.description: Directory paths the worker may READ but NOT modify. Used for context files, type definitions, shared configs that must remain unchanged.

targetHead.description: Which execution backend this contract targets. Determines how the dispatcher spawns the worker: UI queue, headless subprocess, local model, Jules CLI session, or Hermes agent.

budgetLimit.description: Maximum dollar budget for API/model costs for this task. If zero, no budget enforcement. Workers should check this before expensive LLM calls.

localContextMax.description: Maximum context window tokens allocated for this worker. Prevents a single task from exhausting available context on local models.

maxActions.description: Maximum number of tool-call actions this agent may take before forced handoff. Prevents runaway agents.

requiredArtifacts.description: List of artifact types or file paths the worker MUST produce on completion (e.g. ['docs/report.md', 'src/output.json']).

schemaRef.description: URL to a JSON Schema file that the worker's output must validate against. Enables structural contract enforcement at handoff.

expectedFormat.description: Free-text description of the expected output structure — used when a formal schema isn't available.

outputSpec.description: Defines what the worker is expected to produce. Enables the integrator to validate output before merging.

sourceAgent.description: Name of the agent that produced this contract (e.g. 'AGY', 'Hermes', 'SwarmPlanner'). Enables traceability in multi-hop handoffs.

parentThreadId.description: If this contract is a sub-task or refinement of a parent task, link to the parent threadId. Enables thread ancestry tracking via LineageManager.

researchSummary.description: Summary of research performed by the upstream agent (e.g. AGY). Acts as the 'handoff document' — the knowledge the next agent needs but doesn't reprocess.

keyFindings.description: Key findings or decisions from the upstream agent. Bullet-point structured for the downstream agent to consume directly.

path.description: Absolute or repo-relative path to the artifact file.

description.description: Short note about what this artifact contains.

artifactsProduced.description: Artifacts the upstream agent produced that the downstream agent should read before starting.

warnings.description: Warnings, known issues, or pitfalls the downstream agent should be aware of before starting.

handoffContext.description: Context passed from the upstream agent (e.g. AGY) to the downstream implementation agent (e.g. Jules, Hermes). This is the core handoff payload that enables 'AGY researches → Jules implements' workflows.
